audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Judah's descent, Tamar, levirate duty, pledge/recognition motif, Judah's confession, and Perez/Zerah birth. Sensitive material is rendered restrainedly for app use.

verses.restored: At that time Judah went down from his brothers and turned aside to a man of Adullam named Hirah.
Judah saw there the daughter of a Canaanite man named Shua; he took her and came to her.
She conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Er.
She conceived again and bore a son, and she called his name Onan.
She bore still another son and called his name Shelah; Judah was at Chezib when she bore him.
Judah took a woman for Er his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.
Er, Judah’s firstborn, was evil in the eyes of the Eternal, and the Eternal put him to death.
Judah said to Onan, Go to your brother’s woman and perform the brother-in-law duty, and raise up seed for your brother.
Onan knew the seed would not be his, so when he went to his brother’s woman he wasted it, so as not to give seed to his brother.
What he did was evil in the eyes of the Eternal, and he put him to death also.

Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, Remain a widow in your father’s house until Shelah my son grows up, for he said, Lest he also die like his brothers. So Tamar went and dwelled in her father’s house.
Many days passed, and the daughter of Shua, Judah’s woman, died. After Judah was comforted, he went up to his sheep shearers at Timnah, he and Hirah his Adullamite friend.
Tamar was told, Look, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep.

She removed her widow’s garments, covered herself with a veil, wrapped herself, and sat at the entrance of Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah, because she saw that Shelah had grown and she had not been given to him as a woman.
Judah saw her and thought she was a prostitute, because she had covered her face.
He turned aside to her by the road and said, Please let me come to you, for he did not know she was his daughter-in-law. She said, What will you give me, that you may come to me?
He said, I will send a young goat from the flock. She said, Will you give a pledge until you send it?
He said, What pledge shall I give you? She said, Your seal, your cord, and your staff that is in your hand. He gave them to her and came to her, and she conceived by him.
She rose and went away, removed her veil from upon her, and put on her widow’s garments.
Judah sent the young goat by the hand of his Adullamite friend to take the pledge from the woman’s hand, but he did not find her.
He asked the men of her place, Where is the shrine-woman who was at Enaim by the road? They said, No shrine-woman has been here.
He returned to Judah and said, I did not find her; also the men of the place said, No shrine-woman has been here.
Judah said, Let her keep them, lest we become contemptible. Look, I sent this young goat, but you did not find her.
About three months later Judah was told, Tamar your daughter-in-law has played the prostitute, and also look, she is pregnant. Judah said, Bring her out, and let her be burned.
As she was being brought out, she sent to her father-in-law, saying, By the man to whom these belong, I am pregnant. She said, Recognize, please, whose seal, cords, and staff these are.
Judah recognized them and said, She is more righteous than I, because I did not give her to Shelah my son. And he did not know her again.
When the time came for her to give birth, look, twins were in her womb.
As she was giving birth, one put out a hand, and the midwife tied a scarlet thread on his hand, saying, This one came out first.
But as he drew back his hand, look, his brother came out. She said, What a breach you have made for yourself! So his name was called Perez.
Afterward his brother came out, who had the scarlet thread on his hand, and his name was called Zerah.
